2000-01-29.title: Tainted Trust and Mideast Peace
2000-01-29.abstract: Criminal inquiry into campaign practices of Prime Min Ehud Barak threatens to undermine Israelis' trust in him and his ability to make peace with Arabs without compromising security; Barak himself was not implicated by comptroller's report, which found that his associates systematically channeled illegal contributions through nonprofit organizations, but comptroller did hold him responsible for their actions, and a new poll shows that a majority believe Barak was well aware of what they were doing (M)

2000-01-30.title: At Trade Forum, Clinton Pleads for the Poor
2000-01-30.abstract: Pres Clinton appeals to chief executives and national leaders attending World Economic Forum, Davos, Switzerland, to make global trade work for poor; warns failure to listen to concerns of those who have not benefited from globalization would spur protectionism; demonstrators, kept away from forum site, break windows in nearby McDonald's restaurant; forum organizers increase security; photo (M)

2000-01-30.title: Scholar Back in U.S. After China Detention
2000-01-30.abstract: American-based Chinese scholar Song Yongyi arrives back in United States after being jailed in China for nearly six months; holds he was mentally tortured by security agents who demanded he confess to espionage and implicate others, news conference and interview; credits release to pressure from Congress and from western scholars; photo (M)

2000-01-30.title: Mothers Help Sons Outwit Draft Board In Wartime Russia
2000-01-30.abstract: Russian mothers seek advice on strategies to help sons evade draft legally; demand for help has grown with increaded fighting in Chechnya; photo (M)

2000-01-30.title: 2 Tents, Some Chalk: For Nomads, a School
2000-01-30.abstract: Mali, unable to educate most of its children, is encouraging towns and villages to build own primary schools and hire own teachers as lart of larger effort to decentralize power; many community schools are supported by American-financed private groups; community schools, which first appeared in relatively prosperous south, are now opening in north, centering in Timbuktu; map; photos (M)

2000-01-30.title: Ecuador's Coup Alerts Region to a Resurgent Military
2000-01-30.abstract: Other Latin American nations, free of military coups for decade after two centuries of military adventurism, are concerned about whether coup that toppled Ecuador Pres Jamil Mahuad could happen elsewhere in region; groups of military officers all over region are coming under spell of old ideology that posits system in which armed forces bypass traditional political parties and ally themselves directly with masses; passage of time, combined with political squabbling and economic hardship that have accompanied restoration of democratic rule, have softened some of bitter memories of repressive military rule; map; photos (M)

2000-01-31.title: Barak Visits Egypt's Leader To Give Peace Effort a Push
2000-01-31.abstract: Prime Min Ehud Barak of Israel holds three-hour meeting in Cairo, Egypt, with Pres Hosni Mubarak in hope of re-energizing Middle East peace efforts on several fronts; is optimistic stalled negotiations with Syria will soon resume and that new talks with Palestinians, which begin on January 31, will bear fruit; Palestinian negotiators settle in for talks in Jerusalem to bridge wide gulf on difficult issues, such as refugees, status of Jerusalem and contours of possible Palestinian state; Barak has set February 13 deadline for achieving at least framework peace agreement, with further aim of reaching full-fledged treaty by September; photo (M)

2000-01-31.title: U.N. Faces Big Challenge in Any Congo Peacekeeping Mission
2000-01-31.abstract: UN peacekeeping role in Congo, formerly Zaire, remains open question even though African leaders meeting at UN Security Council agree that agency should be given major role; diplomats and UN officials view Congo's complicated war unlike any other organization has faced since disintegration of Balkans into ethnic warfare and collapse of European communism; some major obstacles facing UN discussed; map; photo (M)

2000-01-31.title: Israel-Allied Militia Officer InLebanon Killed By Bomb
2000-01-31.abstract: Guerrillas fighting to oust Israeli troops from southern Lebanon kill Col Akl Hashem, second-most-senior officer of Israeli-backed militia with bomb that exploded near home; Iranian-backed Hezbollah, or Party of God, claims responsibility; Israel vows revenge; accuses Syria, which exercises wide influence in Syria, of showing bad faith in negotiations with Israel by failing to rein in Hezbollah (M)

2000-01-31.title: Clinton Shift on Trade: 'Wake-Up Call'
2000-01-31.abstract: News analysis of Pres Clinton's shift on trade, outlined in speech before world economic forum in Davos, Switzerland; after seven years of pushing harder than any other postwar president to expand trade between US and rest of world, Clinton warned world leaders American people have had enough and have less enthusiasm for exchanging goods and services with world than they did when he first took office; warned new trade deals will get done only if world reinvents way it thinks about trade and globalization to accommodate new American political reality; photo (M)

2000-01-31.title: Buddhist's Escape From Tibet, by Car, Horse and Plane
2000-01-31.abstract: 14-year-old Ugyen Trinley Dorje, 17th Karmapa of Karma Kagyu Buddhist order who fled monastery in Chinese-controlled Tibet in December 1999, was reportedly driven by monks to rugged border area with Nepal and rode unnoticed on horseback before flying to Dharmsala, India; he is only major Tibetan lama recognized as reincarnation of his lineage by both Dalai Lama and Chinese government; map (M)
